pattern: Treating each index as a node with one outgoing edge, a valid loop is a cycle of length greater than one whose every step moves in the same direction; fast/slow pointers detect it while a same-direction check rejects mixed or single-element loops.
hints: Each position points to exactly one next position, so the structure is a functional graph; you are hunting for a cycle, but not every cycle counts.
A cycle is only valid if every index along it moves the same way (all forward or all backward) and it visits more than one distinct index, so a self-loop is rejected.

From each unvisited start, advance a slow and a fast pointer; abandon the walk the moment the direction sign flips or a single-element loop appears, and mark dead-end indices so you never re-explore them.

edge_inputs.description: only a single-element loop exists, which is invalid
one mixed-direction loop is rejected but another valid one exists
single element can only self-loop, never valid
two indices pointing at each other but in opposite directions form no valid loop
movement that lands back on itself is a one-element loop and disqualified
value equal to length wraps to the same index, a self-loop

stress.description: random non-zero values, small length so loops both occur and fail to occur
stress.note: small step magnitudes relative to length make cycles plausible

stress.description: larger array with wider step range to stress same-direction tracking
stress.note: full value range exercises the modular wraparound
